creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Brian from comment #7)

Context: RPi4B Rev 1.5 "C0T" with 8 GiBytes of RAM, microsd card media for
boot testing 14.1-RELEASE based on dd'ing the uncompressed:

http://ftp3.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/ISO-IMAGES/14.1/FreeBSD-14.1-RELEASE-arm64-aarch64-RPI.img.xz

I added to the end of the FreeBSD default config.txt the following
and the rebooted to see what would happen:

[all]
total_mem=1982

It replicated your problem:

uhub1: 4 ports with 4 removable, self powered

mountroot: waiting for device /dev/ufs/rootfs...
Mounting from ufs:/dev/ufs/rootfs failed with error 19.

Loader variables:
  vfs.root.mountfrom=ufs:/dev/ufs/rootfs
  vfs.root.mountfrom.options=rw

Manual root filesystem specification:
  <fstype>:<device> [options]
      Mount <device> using filesystem <fstype>
      and with the specified (optional) option list.

    eg. ufs:/dev/da0s1a
        zfs:zroot/ROOT/default
        cd9660:/dev/cd0 ro
          (which is equivalent to: mount -t cd9660 -o ro /dev/cd0 /)

  ?               List valid disk boot devices
  .               Yield 1 second (for background tasks)
  <empty line>    Abort manual input

mountroot> 

So it appears that the amount of RAM that the RPi* firmware is
configured to use leads to varying the behavior in question.
